pr.title: gr/gm: decompile stage and game functions
pr.author: johnwinston

summary: Decompiled several previously stubbed game-mode and stage functions in gmregclear, grcastle, and gronett, replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes with concrete signatures where supported. The PR reports 100% objdiff matches and main.dol SHA1 verification. The changes mainly clarify Adventure Mode bookkeeping, Hyrule Castle stage animation/render behavior, and Onett ground initialization/callback setup.
what_changed: Implemented fn_8017E318 in gmregclear.c as a loop over 12 lbl_804706D8 entries, summing x2 for entries whose x0 is not -1.

Implemented gm_8017E7FC in gmregclear.c, setting Adventure Mode scene/minor state based on gm_801A4310(), CPU level, elapsed/added value comparison against 0xFD20U, and StartMeleeRules x4_5 fallback behavior.
Implemented gm_80180B18 in gmregclear.c to populate lbl_80472E48.x14 entries from gmMainLib_8015D06C(idx) values divided by 10, using gm_80164024 remapping over 0x1B entries.
Implemented gm_80181A44 in gmregclear.c as a switch over arg1 values 0x21 through 0x26, writing bool flags into offset bands of lbl_803D8D08 indexed by c_kind.
Refined gmregclear local struct layout around lbl_80472D28_t by naming x108 as s16 and exposing byte fields x118, x11A, and x11B instead of padding.

Implemented grCastle_801CE860 in grcastle.c to advance castle stage animation state/timer fields, call grAnime_801C8138, load the next s16 timer from grCs_804D6970 plus an offset, and update the camera via Camera_80030E44.

Implemented fn_801D0924 in grcastle.c as a render/display callback that calls grDisplay_801C5DB0 and, when gp->x10_flags.b7 is set, iterates 12 castle3 data slots and draws non-null entries with lb_800117F4 using two white GXColor values.

Implemented grOnett_801E3CE4 in gronett.c to initialize Onett ground state: joint/map setup, animation setup, ground variable fields, callbacks, a flag, and mpJointSetCb1 callbacks for joints 3 and 4.
Updated headers to replace unknown prototypes for fn_8017E318, gm_80180B18, gm_80181A44, fn_801D0924, and grOnett_801E40E4 with concrete return and parameter types.
smart_moves: Kept header prototypes synchronized with newly matched function bodies, reducing future reliance on UNK_RET and UNK_PARAMS.
Used concrete ground/game data access patterns already present in surrounding code, such as Ground* from gobj->user_data, GET_GROUND, GET_JOBJ, and existing gv union fields.
Preserved matching-sensitive details such as PAD_STACK in grcastle and gronett functions, indicating stack-shape control was required for objdiff parity.
Converted padding into typed fields only where the decompiled code needed direct access, which improves structure knowledge without over-renaming unrelated bytes.
Used explicit pointer arithmetic for grCs_804D6970 timer loading and lbl_803D8D08 offset bands, matching the observed binary layout while avoiding unsupported higher-level struct invention.
Reused existing helper functions for stage initialization and display paths, including grAnime_801C8138, grAnime_801C7FF8, grDisplay_801C5DB0, lb_800117F4, mpJointSetCb1, and Camera_80030E44.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling stage callbacks, matching may require preserving small stack pads near local GXColor or initialization code; do not remove PAD_STACK simply because it appears semantically unnecessary.

Ground gv union fields may be shared or named after other stages, as shown by Onett initialization writing gp->gv.kongo3 and gp->gv.unk fields; evidence should drive naming until the union layout is better understood.
For game-mode tables backed by global byte blobs, a switch with explicit offset bands can be the most evidence-grounded representation before a full struct is recovered.
Function signature cleanup should accompany decompilation: void/int return types and concrete callback parameter lists make later callers and callback registration sites easier to type.
Loop bounds can encode table sizes worth preserving: gmregclear uses 12 entries for lbl_804706D8 and castle3 display data, and 0x1B entries for gm_80164024/gmMainLib records.
Stage animation/timer functions may rely on signed timer decrement behavior; in grCastle_801CE860 the previous timer value is compared after decrementing the stored field.
naming_conventions: Address-based names remain for unknown functions and globals, e.g. fn_8017E318, gm_8017E7FC, gm_80180B18, gm_80181A44, grCastle_801CE860, fn_801D0924, and grOnett_801E3CE4.
Stage functions use the established stage prefix plus address convention, such as grCastle_ and grOnett_.
Game-mode functions use gm_ or fn_ prefixes depending on existing symbol convention and confidence.
Unknown struct fields continue to use offset-style names such as x104, x108, x10A, x116, x118, x11A, x11B, x14, xC4, xC6, xC8, and xCC.
Boolean/bitfield access follows existing names like gp->x10_flags.b5 and gp->x10_flags.b7 rather than inventing semantic names.
Callback prototypes should replace UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS when call sites expose enough evidence, as with grOnett_801E40E4 taking Ground*, s32, CollData*, s32, mpLib_GroundEnum, and f32.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(4) in fn_801D0924 and PAD_STACK(8) in grOnett_801E3CE4 to preserve stack layout for matching.
Used explicit casts and widths, including u8 loop remapping in gm_80180B18, s16 timer handling in grCastle_801CE860, and u32 conversion before division by 10.
Preserved duplicated gm_80164024 calls in gm_80180B18/gm_80180BA0-style code rather than fully simplifying them, likely to maintain instruction ordering/register behavior.
Represented global data tables with pointer arithmetic, such as u8* ptr = (u8*) grCs_804D6970 followed by offset additions and s16 loads.
Used inline GXColor initializer locals in the render callback, which may help match stack object layout and constant materialization.
Used unsigned comparison against 0xFD20U in gm_8017E7FC, preserving signedness-sensitive branch behavior.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body/slice states verification by 100% objdiff match for all functions and main.dol SHA1 OK.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further type lbl_80472D28_t, lbl_80472E48_t, UnkAdventureData, or lbl_803D8D08 in gmregclear.
Search for follow-up work on grCastle_801D09B8 or surrounding Hyrule Castle castle3 render/data structures.
Search for PRs that clarify Onett gv.kongo3/gv.unk field reuse or rename grOnett_801E40E4 callback parameters.
Compare gm_80180B18 and gm_80180BA0 for eventual cleanup of duplicated gm_80164024 calls once matching constraints are understood.
